name: Peru

colors.symbolism: the blood spilled by Peruvian patriots in the struggle for independence
peace, dignity and the purity of the patriotic cause

description: Three equal vertical stripes — red, white, red. The plain civil flag carries no charge; the state flag adds the national coat of arms in the centre of the white band, and the war flag (bandera de guerra) further bears the inscription 'REPUBLICA DEL PERU' around the arms with a wreath.
history: The red-white-red colour scheme was adopted by Liberator José de San Martín in 1820, reportedly inspired by the red and white flamingos he saw on the Peruvian coast. After several intermediate designs, the present vertical-tribar arrangement was established by Decree of 25 February 1825 under the Bolivarian Congress and definitively fixed by the Constitution of 1825; the coat-of-arms version is governed by Law No. 11323 of 31 March 1950, which sets out the three official variants (civil, state and war).

features.symbolism: the vertical bands derive from the bicolour adopted by José de San Martín; the red is traditionally said to recall the blood of patriots in the wars of independence and the white the purity and peace of the new nation
